page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-32 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs, mapped from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB of memory per core. The CPU architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory bandwidth performance, with large-block read-only operations reaching 211.52 GB/sec and write operations at 99.63 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance is also strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 16013.0 and high static web serving throughput of 44.06 GB/sec. However, single-core CPU tasks, software compilation, and database operations perform in the average range.

While the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in image and text processing, it exhibits significant limitations for large-scale AI tasks. Text generation using a 70B parameter LLM is poor, yielding only 2.31 tokens/sec, though smaller 135M model text generation is strong at 296.22 tokens/sec. The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs makes it less optimal for high-I/O databases or heavy deep learning training. The hardware profile is best suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and moderate-scale text or image processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 128 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on network-attached storage and standard CPU execution. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in large-block memory bandwidth, multi-core processing, and static web serving, alongside average single-core and database execution. It supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, which assist in vector and matrix math. However, it exhibits poor performance on large-scale LLM text generation, making it unsuitable for heavy AI inference. This instance is best utilized for general-purpose enterprise workloads, multi-threaded application hosting, static web serving, and moderate data processing tasks where local high-speed storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-32 is a general-purpose x86_64 server with 32 Intel vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-32 is a general-purpose server with 32 Intel vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for balanced multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring thirty-two dedicated Intel vCPUs and strong memory bandwidth for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 128 GB RAM (4 GB per core)
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Strong large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Poor performance for large-scale LLM text generation
Suited for static web serving, text processing, and general-purpose applications
